evidence-binding: n2-probe-machinery 2f0e30aef5ba
## n2-probe-machinery — edition-sync machinery vs #630 prose-generation gap

### edition-sync.js today (rename-only SCRIPT-port generator)
- `scripts/edition-sync.js` (read in full, 1–212) is a script-port generator, NOT a prose generator.
- `GENERATED_AGGREGATORS` (46–55): hard-coded list of exactly 5 canonical `.js` (adaptive-node, next-action, commit-node, adaptive-handoff, plan-validator) — the only files it generates.
- Rename map is DECLARED not derived (`renameSet()` 62–76): scans `plugins/kaola-workflow-<forge>/scripts/` for existing `kaola-<forge>-workflow-(.+)\.js`. Comment (30–35): a derived map would be circular.
- `renderForgePort()` (89–104): the ONLY transform is a bounded regex rename `kaola-workflow-<NAME>` → `kaola-<forge>-workflow-<NAME>` + a `// @generated from …` header. No conditional logic, no section stripping, no frontmatter — pure whole-file find/replace.
- `--check` (`runCheck` 114–140): recomputes `renderForgePort` and does BYTE-FOR-BYTE string compare (`expected !== actual`), reports firstDiff, exits 1. `--write` (155–197): regen 5 forge aggregator ports + byte-copy COMMON_SCRIPTS canonical→codex + BYTE_IDENTICAL_GROUPS.
- CONFIRMS memory: its own comment (24–28) locks scope to "the five forge AGGREGATOR ports — they carry NO forge vocabulary beyond script names." Never touched a `.md`/`SKILL.md`. No notion of runtime (Claude vs Codex) or surface-type (command vs SKILL) — only forge-name divergence within `.js`.
- `scripts/validate-script-sync.js` = the registries edition-sync consumes (COMMON_SCRIPTS, BYTE_IDENTICAL_GROUPS, RENAME_NORMALIZED_FAMILIES, CONFIG_HOOKS_FAMILY, FORGE_EXPORT_SUPERSET_FAMILY) — all script/JSON parity; none include a `.md`/SKILL path.

### Missing machinery for prose generation
1. Skeleton/template format with declared slot boundaries — absent. Closest analog: the #445/D-445-01 skeleton+card design (`docs/conventions.md` 182–199, `docs/plan-run-cards/README.md`) with `<!-- PIN: … -->`/`<!-- CARD: … -->` markers → single-sourced cards under `docs/plan-run-cards/*.md`. But it is a MANUAL authoring convention enforced by hand-copy — nothing renders the six skeleton copies from one source.
2. Conditional (runtime/forge) slot inclusion — the one script that DOES this is `sync-opencode-edition.js` (858 lines, read in full), for a DIFFERENT axis (Claude→opencode): `parseFrontmatter()` (84–94), `transformCommandBody()` (273–332) detects `^##\s+Agent Model Badge`/`Path Intent` headings and skips section-body to next sibling heading, swapping in a runtime block; scoped vocab rewrites (`rewriteClaudeModelNouns`/`rewriteClaudeScriptPaths`); own regenerate-and-byte-compare `--check` (737–810). NONE of this lives in edition-sync.js, and opencode is explicitly additive / NOT one of the six #400 surfaces (D-530-02, conventions.md:375).
3. Forge-vocabulary substitution for PROSE (PR↔MR, glab↔tea, forbidden tokens) — edition-sync only renames script base names; enforced negatively today by `validate-*-contracts.js --forbidden-only`, not generated by construction.
4. md↔toml emission — absent from edition-sync. `.toml` agent-profile triples are byte-identical (validate-script-sync 207–219), generated by the installer, unrelated to command↔SKILL.

### Current drift-detection (the --check seam) — TWO regimes
- SCRIPT drift (edition-sync / sync-opencode): REGENERATE-AND-BYTE-COMPARE (`expected !== actual`). STRONG — any deviation incl. a silently dropped block fails closed. This is the seam #630's "impossible by construction" AC needs — but it exists only for scripts + (out-of-scope) opencode.
- PROSE drift (the six #400 surfaces): NEEDLE/SUBSTRING assertion. `test-route-reachability.js` (497 lines) + `validate-*-contracts.js` do `content.includes(literal)` (T5 `<!-- PIN: frontier unit -->`+literal on all 6 plan-run; T6–T15 similar). `assertIncludes`/`assertNotIncludes`/`assertConcept` per-SKILL. WEAK — only catches a specific pinned literal removed / absent from a new surface; blind to paraphrase, omitted surrounding paragraph, stale example, or any un-pinned content. T5's own comment (137–174) notes this was originally a self-disarming "warn if none" gate, hardened to a hard assert only after #505 found the disarm risk — an admittedly fragile needle-by-needle regime.
- Regeneration is NEVER attempted for prose. conventions.md:74 documents the known failure: a change on only 4-of-6 leaves the two forge-codex SKILLs a "propagation dead zone" (#369, #380 shipped this way); countermeasure is post-hoc token presence, not regen-diff.

### Related generation/sync scripts (confirming the drift class)
- NO existing generator turns `commands/*.md` into `SKILL.md` or vice-versa (grep of scripts/ for template/skeleton/frontmatter/generate → only the walkthrough, resolvers, plan-validator, and the two generators above).
- `validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js` iterates the 11 Codex SKILLs doing needle/assertIncludes per-SKILL → confirms SKILL.md is fully hand-maintained relative to its command.
- conventions.md:74 is the explicit confession of the drift class #630 targets; #400 + the route-reachability test were filed because of exactly this.
- #445 skeleton+card split is the one deliberate design-level mitigation, but it does NOT eliminate the six-way hand-copy of the skeleton nor generate anything.

### Extend-vs-new-engine signal (n2 conclusion)
- edition-sync's CLI SHAPE (`--check`/`--write`, "declared target list + pure render fn + regenerate-and-compare") is a reusable pattern; a new generator kind could sit alongside `GENERATED_AGGREGATORS`.
- edition-sync's RENDER PRIMITIVE (`renderForgePort`, whole-file rename regex) is the WRONG engine for prose (no frontmatter/slots/conditional sections/forge-noun map).
- `sync-opencode-edition.js`'s renderers are the closest working prior art for the render primitives #630 needs — but scoped to a different axis and out of the six #400 surfaces.
- CONCLUSION: building the seam is NOT a small extension of edition-sync as-is — it needs a NEW render engine (closest in shape to sync-opencode's renderers, PLUS a genuinely new forge-noun-substitution + surface-type[command vs SKILL] axis neither script has) glued onto a `--check`/`--write` CLI shell of the kind both existing scripts already ship. WHERE the engine lives (inside edition-sync.js / sibling file / separate module) is a design decision for n4; this probe establishes the rename-transform can't be reused as-is, but the CLI/verification pattern can.

### Key files
- scripts/edition-sync.js — rename-only script-port generator, --check/--write
- scripts/validate-script-sync.js — registries edition-sync consumes (all script/JSON parity)
- scripts/sync-opencode-edition.js — closest prose-gen prior art (frontmatter/section-strip/scoped-rewrite/regen-compare); additive, not a #400 surface
- scripts/test-route-reachability.js — needle/substring verification of the six (weak drift detection)
- scripts/validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js — same needle pattern per Codex SKILL
- docs/conventions.md (62–199, esp. 74, 182–199) — six-surface propagation rule + dead-zone history (#369/#380/#400) + #445 mitigation
- docs/plan-run-cards/README.md + cards — existing skeleton+marker convention (manual, not generated)
- commands/kaola-workflow-plan-run.md — hand-maintained skeleton with CARD markers
- package.json (36,39–40) — sync:editions = edition-sync --write; --check wired only into gitlab/gitea chains, only over scripts

delegation_outcome: completed
